title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Honeybees have developed an extraordinary method of communication (0) known as the waggle dance, which allows them to share information about food. (9) Although the movements may look random to a human observer, they provide precise coordinates to other members of the hive. (10) Such is the accuracy of this dance that it can convey both the distance and the direction of a nectar source. (11) Provided that the sun is visible, the bees use its position as a reference point for their complex aerial choreography. (12) Not only does this behavior ensure the survival of the colony, but it also demonstrates a high level of social intelligence. (13) Regardless of the environmental obstacles they face, foragers are able to update their instructions based on the latest conditions. (14) Not until Karl von Frisch decoded these patterns in the 1940s did the scientific community appreciate the complexity of insect communication. (15) In spite of their tiny brains, bees perform calculations that would require significant effort for many larger animals to complete. (16) Whether this ability is entirely instinctive or partially learned through experience is still a subject of active research today. These insects prove that sophisticated language is not exclusive to mammals.

example.text: Honeybees have developed an extraordinary method of communication (0) known as the waggle dance.
example.explanation: Past participle used in a reduced relative clause.

questions.explanation: Determiner used for emphasis before a noun phrase.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure triggering inversion ('did the... community appreciate').
Part of the linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction introducing a doubt between two alternatives.
